article_title: Takeda's Zasocitinib Beats Deucravacitinib Head-to-Head in Phase 3 Psoriasis Trial, More Than Doubling Complete Skin Clearance
summary: Clinical Phase 3 head-to-head result; every specific verified verbatim against allowlisted BioSpace snapshots; the single automated 'loaded language' warning is the word 'clearly' inside a verbatim direct quote, not editorial prose.

content_preview.title: Takeda's Zasocitinib Beats Deucravacitinib Head-to-Head in Phase 3 Psoriasis Trial, More Than Doubling Complete Skin Clearance
content_preview.summary: In the LATITUDE Atlas trial, more than 35% of patients on Takeda's oral TYK2 inhibitor reached completely clear skin at week 16 — more than 2.5 times the rate seen with Bristol Myers Squibb's deucravacitinib.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Takeda reported on June 11 that its investigational oral drug zasocitinib outperformed deucravacitinib, an already-marketed drug in the same class, in a head-to-head Phase 3 plaque psoriasis trial. More than 35% of patients treated with zasocitinib achieved completely clear skin at week 16 — more than 2.5 times the response rate recorded for deucravacitinib, [according to BioSpace](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/takedas-zasocitinib-significantly-outperforms-deucravacitinib-...

recommendations: No corrections record needed: the lone automated warning is a false positive (the word 'clearly' appears only inside a verbatim attributed quote, not in the article's own voice).

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (830 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clear Overview / What We Know / Background / What We Don't Know / Analysis arc. Appropriately hedged: topline-only data, undisclosed deucravacitinib PASI 100 figure, and pending full dataset are all explicitly noted in the 'What We Don't Know' section.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources are BioSpace (biospace.com, allowlisted). All snapshots fetched with HTTP 200; uncompressed sha256 of each .html.gz matches the manifest (source-0 51eaecc4, source-1 8e0eb371, source-2 0c65a2aa). source-0 (head-to-head release): confirmed verbatim — 'More than 35% ... PASI 100 ... at week 16 - more than 2.5 times the response rate for deucravacitinib', LATITUDE Atlas (TAK-279-PsO-3004 / NCT06973291), 606 participants, zasocitinib 30 mg vs deucravacitinib 6 mg, statistical superiority on 'all key secondary endpoints, including PASI 90 ... sPGA 0', separation 'as early as week 8', 'generally well tolerated ... no new safety signals', NDA filing this fiscal year, '1-million-fold greater selectivity for TYK2', June 11 2026 dateline. Both direct quotes (Linda Stein Gold, Chinwe Ukomadu) reproduced word-for-word. source-1 (placebo/apremilast program): PASI 90 61.3%/51.9% vs placebo 5.0%/4.0%; PASI 100 33.4%/25.2% vs placebo 0.7%/1.1%; PASI 75 at week 4 16.8% vs 4.3% (LATITUDE PsO 3002) — all verbatim. source-2 (Nimbus $4B): $4 billion Nimbus Therapeutics acquisition, Jefferies '$5B by 2030' and 'best-in-class Ph3 results' / 'potential to redefine the oral psoriasis market', Sotyktu brand name — all verbatim. Rule 9/5i satisfied: every clinical number is carried by an allowlisted, fetchable BioSpace snapshot; the non-allowlisted primary Takeda.com / news.bms.com pages were correctly NOT cited.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every clinical specific traces to a cited snapshot. Drug names and endpoints spelled verbatim throughout (zasocitinib, deucravacitinib, TYK2, PASI 100). Rule 2 check: the phrases 'only approved'/'only TYK2 inhibitor' appear in NO source; the article correctly used the softer, sourced descriptors 'already-marketed' and 'established' rather than the unsupported stronger claim. No hallucinated quotes or figures detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No AI self-reference. The single automated 'loaded language' hit ('clearly') is contained entirely within Dr. Stein Gold's attributed verbatim quote and is therefore faithful reproduction, not editorializing. Article prose itself is measured.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Machine Herald coverage of zasocitinib, deucravacitinib, psoriasis, or TYK2 inhibitors. Not a duplicate.
editor_notes.rule9_clinical: Clinical/medical submission reviewed under Rule 9. Trial identifiers (NCT06973291, TAK-279-PsO-3004), endpoint definitions (PASI 100 = 100% reduction on Psoriasis Area and Severity Index), arm sizes, and dosing all verified against the primary press release snapshot. Topline-only nature and undisclosed comparator-arm absolute figure are flagged in-article, avoiding overstatement.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. A clean, accurately sourced clinical News report. All claims trace verbatim to allowlisted, fetchable BioSpace snapshots; quotes are exact; drug-name spelling is correct; the head-to-head superiority claim and the >35% PASI 100 / 2.5x figures are reproduced precisely from the company release. The lone automated warning is a false positive (a word inside a direct quote). No corrections record required.
